description: Main meteorological definitions and formulas that are not part of the units, physics, math, chemistry or geology base data.

words.name: meteorology
words.description: branch of science dealing with the study of atmospheric phenomena and the short term variation of weather conditions including wind, precipitation, temperature, humidity, cloud cover, and air pressure

words.name: climatology
words.description: study of climate

words.name: hydrometeorology
words.description: study of water in the atmosphere

words.name: aeronomy
words.description: science of the upper region of the atmosphere

words.name: atmosphere
words.description: gas layer surrounding Earth

words.name: troposphere
words.description: lowest layer of the atmosphere

words.name: stratosphere
words.description: layer of the atmosphere

words.name: mesosphere
words.description: layer of the atmosphere

words.name: thermosphere
words.description: layer of the Earth's atmosphere above the mesosphere and below the exosphere

words.name: exosphere
words.description: outermost layer of the atmosphere

words.name: tropopause
words.description: upper boundary of troposphere

words.name: advection
words.description: transport of a substance by bulk motion

words.name: insolation
words.description: power per unit area received from the Sun in the form of electromagnetic radiation

words.name: albedo
words.description: ratio of reflected radiation to incident radiation

words.name: ozone
words.description: chemical compound

words.name: aerosol
words.description: colloid of fine solid particles or liquid droplets, in air or another gas

words.name: smog
words.description: severe air pollution

words.name: weather
words.description: state of the atmosphere

words.name: climate
words.description: long-term statistical characterization of atmospheric conditions in a region, typically averaged over periods of 30 years or more

words.name: monsoon
words.description: seasonal changes in atmospheric circulation and precipitation associated with the asymmetric heating of land and sea

words.name: El Nino-Southern Oscillation
words.description: irregularly periodic variation in winds and sea surface temperatures over the tropical eastern Pacific Ocean; see Wikipedia: El Niño–Southern Oscillation (Q14524818)

words.name: cyclone
words.description: large scale air mass that rotates around a strong center of low pressure

words.name: anticyclone
words.description: region where the atmospheric pressure at the surface of the planet is greater than its surrounding environment

words.name: wind
words.description: flow of gases or air on a large scale

words.name: tornado
words.description: violently rotating column of air that is in contact with both the earth's surface and a cumulonimbus cloud in the air

words.name: thunderstorm
words.description: a storm characterized by the presence of lightning, usually accompanied by strong winds and often produce heavy rain and sometimes snow, sleet, or hail, but some produce little precipitation or no precipitation at all

words.name: lightning
words.description: weather phenomenon involving electrostatic discharge

words.name: thunder
words.description: sound caused by a lightning strike

words.name: humidity
words.description: amount of water vapor in the air

words.name: precipitation
words.description: liquid or solid water that falls to the ground

words.name: rain
words.description: liquid water in the form of droplets that have condensed from atmospheric water vapor and then precipitated

words.name: snow
words.description: precipitation in the form of ice crystal flakes

words.name: hail
words.description: type of precipitation formed out of ice

words.name: drizzle
words.description: light liquid precipitation

words.name: condensation
words.description: change of the physical state of matter from gas phase into liquid phase; reverse of evaporation

words.name: evaporation
words.description: type of vaporization of a liquid that occurs from its surface; surface phenomenon

words.name: sublimation
words.description: transition of a substance directly from the solid to the gas state

words.name: transpiration
words.description: process of water moving through a plant and its evaporation from aerial parts

words.name: cloud
words.description: visible mass of liquid droplets or frozen crystals suspended in the atmosphere

words.name: fog
words.description: atmospheric phenomenon

words.name: mist
words.description: phenomenon caused by small droplets of water suspended in air

words.name: evapotranspiration
words.description: biophysicogeochemical process

words.name: dew
words.description: water in the form of droplets that appears on thin, exposed objects in the morning or evening

words.name: frost
words.description: coating or deposit of ice that may form in humid air in cold conditions, usually overnight

words.name: visibility
words.description: in meteorology, distance at which an object or light can be clearly discerned

words.name: barometer
words.description: scientific instrument used to measure atmospheric pressure

words.name: thermometer
words.description: device that measures temperature or a temperature gradient

words.name: hygrometer
words.description: instrument used for measuring the moisture content in the atmosphere

words.name: anemometer
words.description: meteorological instrumentation used for measuring the speed of wind

words.name: radiosonde
words.description: meteorological instrumentation

words.name: latitude
words.description: angle between zenith and a plane parallel to the equator

words.name: altitude
words.description: height in relation to a certain reference point

words.name: El Niño
words.description: the warm phase of the El Niño–Southern Oscillation, with warmer sea surface temperatures in the central and eastern Pacific

words.name: La Niña
words.description: the cool phase of the El Niño–Southern Oscillation, with cooler sea surface temperatures in the central and eastern Pacific

triples.name: atmospheric physics
triples.description: application of physics to the study of the atmosphere. see Wikipedia: Atmospheric physics (Q5334416)

triples.name: atmospheric chemistry
triples.description: branch of atmospheric science. see Wikipedia: Atmospheric chemistry (Q287919)

triples.name: synoptic meteorology
triples.description: 1000-km-order method of measuring weather systems. see Wikipedia: Synoptic scale meteorology (Q1233837)

triples.name: dynamic meteorology
triples.description: branch of science dealing with the study of atmospheric phenomena and the short term variation of weather conditions including wind, precipitation, temperature, humidity, cloud cover, and air pressure. see Wikipedia: Atmospheric dynamics (Q25261)

triples.name: weather forecasting
triples.description: application of science and technology to predict the conditions of the atmosphere for a given location and time. see Wikipedia: Weather forecasting (Q182868)

triples.name: atmospheric pressure
triples.description: static pressure exerted by the weight of the atmosphere. see Wikipedia: Atmospheric pressure (Q81809)

triples.name: pressure gradient force
triples.description: physical force from a pressure differential. see Wikipedia: Pressure-gradient force (Q1541234)

triples.name: lapse rate
triples.description: vertical rate of change of temperature in atmosphere. see Wikipedia: Lapse rate (Q66900467)

triples.name: temperature inversion
triples.description: deviation from the normal change of an atmospheric property with altitude. see Wikipedia: Inversion (meteorology) (Q190096)

triples.name: adiabatic process
triples.description: thermodynamic process with zero heat transfer. see Wikipedia: Adiabatic process (Q182453)

triples.name: atmospheric convection
triples.description: atmospheric phenomenon. see Wikipedia: Atmospheric convection (Q931652)

triples.name: potential temperature
triples.description: temperature that a fluid would attain if adiabatically brought to a standard reference pressure. see Wikipedia: Potential temperature (Q760765)

triples.name: solar irradiance
triples.description: power per unit area received from the Sun in the form of electromagnetic radiation. see Wikipedia: Solar irradiance (Q7556707)

triples.name: greenhouse effect
triples.description: atmospheric heat retention. see Wikipedia: Greenhouse effect (Q41560)

triples.name: outgoing longwave radiation
triples.description: electromagnetic radiation of wavelengths between 3.0 and 100 μm emitted from Earth and its atmosphere out to space in the form of thermal radiation. see Wikipedia: Outgoing longwave radiation (Q2999493)

triples.name: shortwave radiation
triples.description: type of radiant energy. see Wikipedia: Shortwave radiation (Q7502259)

triples.name: solar constant
triples.description: intensity of sunlight. see Wikipedia: Solar constant (Q335272)

triples.name: Coriolis force
triples.description: apparent or fictitious force on objects moving within a reference frame that rotates with respect to an inertial frame. see Wikipedia: Coriolis force (Q169973)

triples.name: geostrophic wind
triples.description: theoretical wind that would result from an exact balance between the Coriolis force and the pressure gradient force. see Wikipedia: Geostrophic wind (Q929043)

triples.name: ozone layer
triples.description: region of Earth's stratosphere that absorbs most of the Sun's UV radiation. see Wikipedia: Ozone layer (Q79995)

triples.name: water cycle
triples.description: continuous movement of water on, above and below the surface of the Earth. see Wikipedia: Water cycle (Q81041)

triples.name: air mass
triples.description: widespread body of air approximately homogeneous in its horizontal extent with vertical temperature and moisture variations that are approximately the same over its horizontal extent. see Wikipedia: Air mass (Q216823)

triples.name: weather front
triples.description: boundary separating two masses of air of different densities. see Wikipedia: Weather front (Q189796)

triples.name: cold front
triples.description: leading edge of a cooler mass of air. see Wikipedia: Cold front (Q210316)

triples.name: warm front
triples.description: boundary of advancing mass of warm air. see Wikipedia: Warm front (Q537536)

triples.name: occluded front
triples.description: part of cyclone formation. see Wikipedia: Occluded front (Q747330)

triples.name: stationary front
triples.description: weather front. see Wikipedia: Stationary front (Q1194255)

triples.name: wind speed
triples.description: measure. see Wikipedia: Wind speed (Q1464943)

triples.name: wind direction
triples.description: geographic direction from which a wind blows. see Wikipedia: Wind direction (Q1057900)

triples.name: jet stream
triples.description: fast-flowing atmospheric air-current. see Wikipedia: Jet stream (Q202325)

triples.name: trade winds
triples.description: permanent east-to-west prevailing winds that flow in the Earth's equatorial region. see Wikipedia: Trade winds (Q160603)

triples.name: tropical cyclone
triples.description: rapidly rotating storm system that forms in the tropics. see Wikipedia: Tropical cyclone (Q8092)

triples.name: relative humidity
triples.description: amount of water vapor in the air. see Wikipedia: Humidity (Q180600)

triples.name: specific humidity
triples.description: amount of water vapor in the air. see Wikipedia: Specific humidity (Q180600)

triples.name: mixing ratio
triples.description: abundance of one component of a mixture relative to others. see Wikipedia: Mixing ratio (Q171293)

triples.name: dew point
triples.description: temperature at which air becomes saturated with water vapour. see Wikipedia: Dew point (Q178828)

triples.name: water vapor
triples.description: gaseous phase of water; unlike other forms of water, water vapor is invisible. see Wikipedia: Water vapor (Q190120)

triples.name: vapor pressure
triples.description: pressure exterted by a vapor in thermodynamic equilibrium. see Wikipedia: Vapor pressure (Q2661322)

triples.name: cumulus cloud
triples.description: genus of clouds, low-level cloud. see Wikipedia: Cumulus cloud (Q14189)

triples.name: stratus cloud
triples.description: type of cloud. see Wikipedia: Stratus cloud (Q40526)

triples.name: cirrus cloud
triples.description: cloud type. see Wikipedia: Cirrus cloud (Q185638)

triples.name: cumulonimbus cloud
triples.description: genus of clouds, dense towering vertical cloud associated with thunderstorms and atmospheric instability. see Wikipedia: Cumulonimbus cloud (Q182311)

triples.name: surface runoff
triples.description: flow of excess rainwater not infiltrating in the ground over its surface. see Wikipedia: Surface runoff (Q1444790)

triples.name: heat index
triples.description: temperature index that accounts for the effects of humidity. see Wikipedia: Heat index (Q2141844)

triples.name: wind chill
triples.description: lowering of body temperature due to the passing-flow of lower-temperature air. see Wikipedia: Wind chill (Q1044340)

triples.name: rain gauge
triples.description: meteorological instrumentation. see Wikipedia: Rain gauge (Q190052)

triples.name: weather balloon
triples.description: meteorological instrumentation. see Wikipedia: Weather balloon (Q1128180)

triples.name: weather station
triples.description: set of sensors that record and provide physical measurements and meteorological parameters. see Wikipedia: Weather station (Q190107)

triples.name: standard atmospheric pressure
triples.description: physical conditions at sea level. see Wikipedia: Standard sea-level conditions (Q7598376)

triples.name: saturation vapor pressure
triples.description: the vapor pressure at which air is saturated with water vapor at a given temperature

triples.name: Coriolis parameter
triples.description: twice the vertical component of the Earth's angular velocity, used in the equations of atmospheric motion
the rate of rotation of the Earth about its axis, about 7.292e-5 radians per second
the mass of water vapor contained in a parcel of air
the mass of the dry air, excluding water vapor, in a parcel of air
the total mass of a parcel of moist air including its water vapor

triples.name: reflected radiation
triples.description: the amount of radiation reflected by a surface

triples.name: incoming radiation
triples.description: the amount of radiation arriving at a surface
the air temperature expressed in degrees Celsius

triples.name: temperature difference
triples.description: the difference in temperature between two points

triples.name: height difference
triples.description: the difference in height between two points

triples.name: dry adiabatic lapse rate
triples.description: the rate at which an unsaturated air parcel cools as it rises, about 9.8 K per kilometre

triples.name: environmental lapse rate
triples.description: the actual rate at which air temperature decreases with height in the atmosphere

formulas.name: definition of mixing ratio
formulas.expression: "mixing ratio" = "mass of water vapor" / "mass of dry air"

formulas.name: definition of specific humidity
formulas.expression: "specific humidity" = "mass of water vapor" / "mass of moist air"

formulas.name: definition of saturation vapor pressure
formulas.expression: "saturation vapor pressure" = 6.112 * exp(17.67 * "temperature in celsius" / ( "temperature in celsius" + 243.5 ))

sources.name: by definition
sources.description: a value that is fixed by an unambiguous definition or convention rather than measured

sources.name: meteorology reference data
sources.description: typical reference values for the atmosphere as cited in meteorology textbooks
